reason: IS-IS, OSPF and RIP are all interior gateway protocols (BGP is the exterior one), so 'All of them'.

Deadlock = set of processes each blocked waiting on a resource held by another, so none ever proceeds.

RISC = small set of simple, fixed-length, single-cycle instructions; memory operands and large instruction counts are CISC traits.

An ARP request is sent to the Ethernet broadcast address FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF = all 1's (the unknown target hardware field inside the payload is all 0's, so the stem is loose but the intended answer is all 1's).

blindAnswer: A node holds a data field plus a pointer/link to the next node (and to the previous node in a doubly linked list); the chain of next-pointers from the head is what gives the list its structure and allows traversal.
reason: Standard definition of a linked-list node: data + link to next node.
sourceAnswer: Each element in a linked list is called a node. A single node contains data and a pointer to the next node which helps in maintaining the structure of the list. The first node is called the head; it points to the first node of the list and helps us access every other element in the list.

blindAnswer: Moore: outputs depend only on the current state. Mealy: outputs depend on the current state AND the current inputs (so Mealy reacts a cycle earlier and usually needs fewer states, while Moore outputs are glitch-free/registered).
reason: Textbook distinction between Moore and Mealy output functions.
sourceAnswer: Moore depends on present state, Mealy depends on present state and present input.

reason: Well-known TCP port for HTTP is 80.

blindAnswer: Translation Lookaside Buffer - a small associative cache of recent virtual-to-physical page translations, used so the MMU can avoid walking the page table in memory on every access.
reason: TLB caches page-table entries to speed up address translation.
sourceAnswer: Translation Look Aside Buffer: memory cache that is used to reduce the time taken to access a user memory location.

blindAnswer: Binary search trees and self-balancing/multi-way variants: BST, AVL tree, Red-Black tree, Splay tree, Treap, 2-3 tree, B-tree and B+ tree (m-way search trees), plus specialized ones like ternary search trees.
reason: Open-ended; the standard taxonomy is binary search trees (BST, AVL, red-black, splay, treap) and m-way search trees (2-3, B-tree, B+ tree).
